original_sentence: Seriously, I don’t want to meet new people just for this fucking reason. I got into an argument with my sister’s boyfriend because he got mad that I intervene because I got scare that he was gonna hit my sister. They were in a room fighting pretty bad, screaming and he sounded very intimidating and like he was hitting or about to hit her. I don’t like abusive people, I don’t fucking care if you get angry because I interrupted your abusive spree, fuck off. I’ve seen them for months and I recognize the red flags in him. I’ve been around this kind of people before. The thing that I’m upset about is that he tried to gaslight us into making me the problem for knocking on their door and deflect from his behavior. I don’t know how to go into detail right now, I don’t know how to start, I’m not use to venting on here and it will probably be too long. But long story short he made it clear that he doesn’t want me to intervene when that happens again. I was trying to say to them that that should not happen again but it was more important to him that I didn’t intervene? Like, make it make sense. Anyway, my sister is in her early 20’s and he is in his 30’s, like 13 years older than her. She is very strong but has a lot of childhood trauma unresolved. They’ve been living together for less than a year, not long after starting dating and for reasons that I won’t get into, I ended up being their roommate a few months later. And what they don’t understand is that if they hurt each other I’m responsable too because I live here. I don’t mess with their relationship, I’ve never said anything about them as a couple, but things have gotten to far, they fight more frequent, aggressive and I was already thinking about talking to them if posible or just her after the holidays about how dangerous it is what they’re doing, but I didn’t realize that he was just pretending to be nice and understanding about this topics but in fact my intuition was right about him, I didn’t know him because she stared dating him so soon without telling anyone. Is so sad because I really want to believe and see the best in people. I think she doesn’t want to talk to me because I’m very direct and don’t sugarcoat things if something is this bad. This relationship is going to destroy her and I can’t do anything. I’m always the one to want to talk, give advice, and help, but I won’t do that anymore, I’m on the end of my rope here. I’ve learned that if someone doesn’t want help, you have to respect that. So, another red flag is that he hits his dog and her cat if they do something he doesn’t like and she allows that! I didn’t realized she could stay with a person that does that, I just can’t believe it. I feel like I don’t know my sister anymore. And he want to get her pregnant soon because he says he is getting old, like? Dude, you don’t even help around the house or cook, and she works too. There’s other things that happened that day that were not ok of him, but i don’t have the energy to get in to. That was the first time that we got into an argument like that and he responds that way? I was flabbergasted and shocked that he didn’t give me the same respect, consideration and benefit of the doubt that I’ve given him all this time. I’m going to gray rock them both because i don’t feel safe being open to them anymore, I’m distancing myself emotionally because it is just to much on my mental health. I’ve been trough some difficult time myself on this past years and I’m staring to see some light, finally. I just hope that she wakes up from this illusion, because I don’t want her to end up with an abusive husband and father of her future children. And I’m looking for a place to move hopefully soon. I just feel helpless seeing this and not be able to do anything because I can’t force her to go. I need to accept that she is an adult and I can’t keep saving here from abusive people and putting my well being on the line for her anymore. I’m her older sister, I’ve been doing that since she was born but it has been hell for me. I love her and I’m letting her go. I’m sad that if this continues I won’t be part of my sisters life anymore because I’m not willing to accept people in my life that don’t reciprocate the love that I give, no more one sided relationships for ma and I can’t see someone I love suffer and not wanting help either. I know maybe this sounds harsh but is what I have to do right now.

original_sentence: We started going to a couples' therapist about 4 months ago, and recently, things started slowly getting better. I don't actually think the therapist is any good-- she is pretty inexperienced and deals in cliches, but doing this once a week has forced us to start communicating more. Two weeks ago we had a pretty bad fight: voices raised, yelling, both not hearing the other etc. I ended up writing a letter to my husband and not sending it. Then this past weekend, I sat down and read it aloud (most of it, except the parts said in anger), he listened and we talked about things for hours. I opened doors and windows we had been ignoring-- like our lackluster sex life (that we BOTH have libido issues now), fears about us having children when we still have a lot to work on in our own relationship, and my own mental health struggles with my parents and lack of self-esteem. He really listened and did not try to interrupt or get defensive.

original_sentence: I am in college and live with my mom. She has depressive episodes, lives in a house she can’t afford, and has no income. I want to keep an eye on her, help pay for living expenses, and be around for company since her family is on the other side of the country. I’ve had a hard past few years after covid with friendships and starting over at college, so I’ve had a hard enough time keeping myself functional. I really wanted a dog to give us something for emotional support, unconditional loving, something to keep us active and on a proper schedule. My mom now gets so upset when I was study for finals and now, went away for a day and a half for Christmas, since my parents are divorces and I was scheduled to be with my dad’s side of the family for this holiday. I also have another week long vacation scheduled with my dad this month. She blames that she can’t find a job, insurance, or sell the house while also taking care of the dog. I offered that we could keep the dog at a doggy hotel, or my dad’s family could care for the dog. I’m caring for the dog all day during the winter break so far, and she comes and goes to care for the dog as she likes. I wake up in the night to take the puppy out to pee, I wake up in the morning at 6 am or 7 am to care for the dog, stay in the living room with her until 9 pm when I stay with her until she goes down for the night. The only time my mom is left to help is if she wants to stay up in the living room/kitchen, which is the dogs space, then she has to put the dog to sleep since it’s past the schedule. My mom cares, feeds, and plays with the puppy because she wants to. I’m not forcing or leaving the dog unattended, giving my mom a terrible burden. But still the dog and me are crippling her from achieving what she needs to do. She could have found a job for the past 10 years, sold the house for the past 3 years, but she has no incentive to do these things. She has had bouts where she locks herself in her room for days only coming out at night to eat, where I couldn’t see her for almost a week. But now she is so hateful, mad and yells that I’m selfish, spoiled, and unresponsible for having this puppy. Her sleep and eating schedule has rapidly improved from nocturnal (which is better for job hunting), she hasn’t locked herself in her room in bed all day since getting the dog, and she has company when I have commitments with the other side of my family/this vacation (where as normally she would have a depressive episode). My mom feels so wronged by me and says the most hurtful things. I feel hurt and wronged when I went though all the trouble of finding/contacting/paying for a good healthy puppy that is her favorite breed. Then, I found and made appointments with the vet. And now, I’m finding a puppy school under my mom’s urging that “we should enroll the dog in a puppy school soon, I hope they all aren’t booked up”. We both know my mom gets nothing done and I will have to find the school. It feels like shes hatefully spewing at me in the hopes that I will sell the house, find her a job, and insurance. If she hurts and prods at me enough, I or by proxy my dad will solve all her problems. I just want it to stop, I find caring for the puppy a break from school work, but I guess my mom considers the puppy work. I feel like I’m being constantly slapped after giving the best gift I could think of.

I’ve had a hard past few years after covid with friendships and starting over at college,
I’m caring for the dog all day
I just want it to stop,
I feel like I’m being constantly slapped

original_sentence: In the past, even though I haven't played the games, I had identified with Bridget as a character because of being a crossdresser. I felt bitter about Bridget's gender, because it felt like the developers had taken away gender nonconforming representation and hastily given subpar representation to trans people in return. Now though, I've looked deeper into her story, and found that it was planned from the beginning, and pretty well done. It actually seems like quite a deep, realistic, and even beautiful story about choosing to be who you are for yourself. I'm still partly worried about if it sends the message that not conforming to gender roles means you're secretly trans, but I don't really think it does. I spent a long couple weeks stressed out about the whole thing before I took a deep dive into her story and found out how it really was, and I'm not feeling fully OK again, but I know logically it's all good now. I still feel some sense of loss and anger because Bridget was one of the only crossdressing protagonists in games originally, and there still aren't that many, and there being others still wouldn't erase the feeling of loss. I don't really know how to respond to that feeling, but if anyone has some consolation to give me, I'd appreciate your help a lot.

original_sentence: I was in a 5-year long-term relationship. He was my everything, we saw each other every day, we spoke every single second of every day we were inseparable. I believe about a year prior to the ending of the relationship things started to turmoil. No matter how hard I tried, no matter what I did in his eyes I could tell I was never enough. He never complimented me, never made effort to give me physical affection, never planned dates or paid for any time we went out, it was such a one-sided relationship. Yet, no matter what I was so in love and so unbelievably happy with him that I fought myself to make it work so hard. I wish things ended years ago. I wish I had something happen that would've made me turn around and dump his ass. I think it's been idk 6 months now and I have completely lost who I am as an individual. He ended it, told me he no longer loved me and we will never be happy in the future. I lost my identity, I lost my happiness, I lost someone who I thought I would end up with for the rest of my life. How does one person cope with this loss? I understand you go through your healing journey, you write, you do self-care, you go out a lot with your friends and make new memories and you spend a lot of time on your own, but how do you heal the constant dulling pain in your chest? I had to remove him from my social media, I had to delete friends, and family because my heart couldn't take the pain. Yet, when I hear his name or see a recent photo I lose it. How could someone I loved so much, give so much of myself to do this to me? I question myself every single day what went wrong, why did my heart break so horribly by the one person who promised me he would love me for the rest of my life? Why does no one talk about the constant feeling of emptiness and sadness you have trying to rebuild the new life that you so badly wanted them to be a part of? The fact that hurts the most is that he could forget me in a matter of days, weeks, and months and appear to be completely unbothered and fine- why am I the one who is suffering so badly? I will never ever understand why this happened, what I ever did to deserve this to happen to me? I did nothing but love, admire and respect him and treat him the way I could. It just hurts. I am upset all the time, I have to hide it all the time and I'm frigging tired. Pretending to be ok is exhausting. I guess the moral of the story is, I am sorry for those who are also feeling this way, I get you. I wish somebody could understand even for a second how this feels.

original_sentence: PTSD causes me to shut down in stressful events. I'm not a doctor by any means, but I think that you're shutting down because your brain is trying to keep yourself alive in what it sees as a threatening situation. It shuts down your fight or flight and essentially stops "recording" so that you can focus on your survival. I view my trauma like I'm watching a movie because my brain fully disconnected me from the experiences. Triggers cause my brain to return to that moment, attached with the emotions I should have felt then. Having flashbacks of traumatic events months after the event is usually how people discover that they have PTSD, because suddenly that apathetic disconnect becomes overwhelmingly personal. I wish you the best and it can be helpful to learn PTSD coping skills for any kind of trauma whether it causes PTSD or not. Good luck and stay safe.

original_sentence: I was on the 2 year contract it’s 2 years active 4 years reserve 2 years inactive reserve. I could get out in two months but instead loved my experience as a non rate (Aton work) and am heading to a school next month. To go to a school I extended for 2 more years to make it 4 years total. You don’t have to re enlist you can just extend over and over. Getting out with 2 years is not a bad option to chase school but once you get in you may love it that’s my experience!

original_sentence: Well Christmas is coming & she hates Christmas. When SD was only 4 years old she told her Santa wasn’t real & it crushed her (I know how some people feel about Santa but geez let her be a kid if she’s enjoying Santa I don’t understand purposely just saying NO HES NOT REAL at FOUR without even discussing it with SO) so that took all the magic out of it. She felt like she couldn’t do any Christmas stuff bc he wasn’t real, but SD asked if we could still pretend he was real & of course we said yes! The latest issue is BM told us we need to stop doing presents & celebrating all together because it’s a religious holiday (we don’t celebrate it religiously & she knows that) & we need to teach her about giving instead. She said we need to stop “throwing money at her” every year by buying her Xmas presents. Not that we have anything to prove to her but SD goes through her toys every year before her bday & again before Christmas to pick things out to give away to make room for new toys. She’s not spoiled and I see Xmas as a time to celebrate how well she’s done in all her activities and school all year. Plus we spend lots of time celebrating with family & our own traditions. She deserves everything she’s getting and it’s not all about presents. Plus we celebrate Kwanzaa which is all about giving & appreciation.

original_sentence: Usually the guideline for long acting to basal setting conversion should be similar to your TDD of basal. For example: I was on 20 units Lantus and now my basal rate averages about 1.0u/h making my TDD about 24 units. They started me off similarly as well, around 0.8u/h and slowly increased it over 2 weeks to avoid lows and to ensure it wasn’t too much.
